special: Gets +10 meters per hit when Sprinting.
Spirit of Air.description: An exceptionally fast and agile spirit whose core powers emphasize mobility, concealment, confusion, engulfing targets, and search. Its optional powers can add elemental offense, protection, fear, toxic breath, or psychokinesis.

Spirit of Beasts.description: A physically powerful and perceptive spirit associated with animals. Its standard abilities support animal control, enhanced senses, fear, movement, and close combat, while its optional powers can add concealment, protection, natural weaponry, toxic breath, tracking, or venom.

Spirit of Earth.description: A slow but extremely tough and strong spirit. Its core abilities emphasize binding, protection, movement, and search, with optional access to concealment, confusion, engulfing attacks, elemental offense, or fear.

special: Gets +5 meters per hit when Sprinting.
Spirit of Fire.description: A quick offensive spirit with built-in elemental attack, energy aura, engulf, confusion, and accident powers. Its optional powers can add fear, protection, toxic breath, or search, while its severe allergy to water is a major vulnerability.

optional_powers: Innate Spell (any one spell known by the summoner; Force is limited to the spirit's Magic)
Spirit of Man.description: A versatile spirit associated with metahumanity and civilization. Its standard abilities combine concealment, confusion, protection, influence, enhanced vision, and search, while its optional powers can provide fear, movement, psychokinesis, or an innate spell known by the summoner.

special: Moves twice as fast when in water.
Spirit of Water.description: A mobile and versatile spirit with concealment, confusion, engulf, movement, and search among its standard powers. Its optional powers can add accident, binding, elemental offense, an energy aura, protection, or weather control. It moves twice as fast in water but has a severe allergy to fire.

category.Spirits:
Spirits
Spirits are denizens of the metaplanes. The Shadowrun 5e Core Rulebook provides six spirit types: Air, Beasts, Earth, Fire, Man, and Water.

Force
A spirit's statistics are calculated from its Force (F). Physical attributes are used when the spirit is materialized on the physical plane, and an attribute has a minimum rating of 1 even when its formula would produce a lower value.

Skills
A spirit has each listed skill at a rating equal to its Force.

Movement
On the physical plane, a spirit's Walking rate is Agility × 2 and its Running rate is Agility × 4. Spirits can move in any direction and ignore gravity. A spirit uses Running to Sprint for +2 meters per hit, except where a spirit type has a special movement rule.

Optional Powers
A spirit gains one optional power for every 3 full points of Force. The chosen optional powers do not change.

Edge
Summoned and bound spirits do not use their own Edge pools. A summoner may spend their own Edge on tests made by their summoned spirits.
